I fully agree with the initial conclusion. In addition, recent studies confirm that endothelial cell-derived vWF plays a critical role in maintaining hemostasis under normal conditions, particularly through its ability to stabilize Factor VIII and mediate platelet adhesion at sites of vessel damage.

[End of conversation]

I fully agree with the initial conclusion. Additionally, while platelets can transiently bind and concentrate vWF at sites of injury, this occurs *after* endothelial secretion—platelets do not generate or secrete vWF de novo. This reinforces that endothelial cells remain the sole physiological source of vWF synthesis and release.  

Final Answer: **B**.
